items.hook: What we’re reading: writers and readers on the books they enjoyed in June
items.body:
Candice Carty-Williams, Patrick Freyne and Guardian readers discuss the titles they have read over the last month. Join the conversation in the comments

I just finished reading Wimmy Road Boyz by Sufiyaan Salam. I

items.hook: The Treaties of Orebro end both the Anglo-Russian and Anglo-Swedish Wars.
items.body: Two Treaties of Örebro were signed on the same day, 18 July 1812, in Örebro, Sweden. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Using AI for creative pursuits? Moderation is key
items.body: An experiment showed AI users had the most creative ideas when they used it in moderation – not too much and not too little. Columnist David Robson puts the finding to the test, and explores what we lose when we over-rely on AI

items.hook: The Aurora Awards are granted annually by the Canadian SF and Fantasy Association and SFSF Boreal Inc.
items.body: The Award for Best Graphic Novel was first awarded in 2011 as a category for both the English-language awards and introduced to the French-language awards in 2017. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The novel in Scotland includes all long prose fiction published in Scotland and by Scottish authors since the development of the literary format in the eighteenth century.
items.body: The novel was soon a major element of Scottish literary and critical life. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Chinese forces under general Li Shiji besiege the strategic fortress city of Anshi (Liaoning) during the Goguryeo–Tang War.
items.body: Li Shiji, courtesy name Maogong, posthumously known as Duke Zhenwu of Ying, was a Chinese military general and politician who lived in the early Tang dynasty. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Red Mouth by Sheila Armstrong review – profound exploration of Ireland’s deep time
items.body:
Sinister bogland discoveries haunt the intersecting lives of four characters in this meditative, exquisitely written tale

Almost 14% of Ireland is bog: vast swathes of moss-carpeted land, below which layers of ancient history have

items.hook: Bayeux Tapestry in 'wonderful state' after journey to London, French minister says
items.body: The French culture minister has told the BBC that the Bayeux Tapestry is in "a wonderful state".

items.hook: ESA’s Mars Express meets NASA’s Psyche
‘Colony’ director Yeon Sang-ho talks sequel plans through novel, video game
items.body: EXCLUSIVE: The film has proved a box office hit in South Korea and is set for a US release on August 28.

items.hook: Storms provoke severe flooding on the Saguenay River, beginning one of Quebec's costliest natural disasters ever.
items.body: The Saguenay flood was a series of flash floods on July 19 and 20, 1996 that hit the Saguenay–Lac-Saint-Jean region of Quebec, Canada. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: It may refer to blocking of sunlight by a roof, a tree, an umbrella, a window shade or blind, wall, curtains, or other objects.
items.body: Shade is the blocking of sunlight by any object, and also the shadow created by that object. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Intel is founded in Mountain View, California.
items.body: Intel Corporation is an American multinational technology company headquartered in Santa Clara, California. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Secret Life of Bees is a novel by American author Sue Monk Kidd.
items.body: Set in 1964, it is a coming-of-age story about loss, betrayal, and the interracial landscape of the civil-rights era of the American South. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: A landslide occurs on the Iliwerung volcano in Indonesia, triggering a tsunami that kills over 530 and leaves 700 missing.
items.body: Iliwerung or Illiwerung is a complex volcano forming a prominent south-facing peninsula on Lembata Island in southern Indonesia. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: On the Road with the Archangel is the thirteenth novel by the American author and theologian, Frederick Buechner.
items.body: The novel was first published in 1997 by Harper, San Francisco. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Minea is both a surname and feminine given name, of Romanian and Finnish origin, respectively.
items.body: The given name, being unknown in the wider world, was probably invented by Finnish writer Mika Waltari in his 1945 novel The Egyptian, who is believed to have based it off of the name of the mythical Greek king Minos. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Louis Faidherbe, French governor of Senegal, arrives to relieve French forces at Kayes, effectively ending El Hajj Umar Tall's war against the French.
items.body: Louis Léon César Faidherbe was a French general and colonial administrator. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Brenda Fricker, Oscar winner for My Left Foot, dies aged 81
items.body:
The acclaimed Irish actor started her career in Coronation Street and Casualty before a string of high-profile Hollywood roles

Brenda Fricker, who became the first female Irish Oscar winner for acting with My Left Foot, has died ag
